reasoning: The Federal Funds rate is currently at 4.00%. The FOMC is overwhelmingly expected (~87% chance) to cut the rate by 25 basis points at their next meeting on December 9-10, 2025, or at least keep it flat. The chances of a rate hike are essentially zero. For the subsequent resolution dates of Jan 6, 2026 (no meeting in between) and Mar 7, 2026 (after the Jan 28-29, 2026 meeting), the broader macroeconomic context remains an easing cycle. Although uncertainty slightly increases over time, the prospect of an abrupt pivot to rate hikes within this 3-month horizon is negligible. Therefore, probabilities are set to the near-minimum possible values, slightly increasing for more distant dates.
